title: Saudi News: 13,500-year-old settlement found in Nefud Desert, 190km trade links stun archaeologists
summary_full: Archaeologists in Saudi Arabia uncovered a 13,500-year-old human settlement, revealing sophisticated tools and evidence of organized hunter-gatherer communities. This discovery pushes back the timeline of human history in the Arabian Peninsula, demonstrating a thriving desert society with long-term occupation and extensive trade networks, challenging previous notions of the region's marginal role in early human development.
